div.p:
Why memchr() not strchr():
strchr() stops at \0; the buffer may contain null bytes beyond the request line. memchr(ptr, ch, len) scans exactly len bytes — safe.

Why (unsigned char) casts matter:
Any ctype.h call on a signed char with value > 127 is UB. Always cast: tolower((unsigned char)c).

Pointer arithmetic safety:
method_len = first_space − line — difference is always positive and bounded by line_len.

✅ first_space → index 3 method_len = first_space − line = 3 − 0 = **3** Guard: method_len == 0 → false
Guard: method_len >= MAX_METHOD_LEN (16) → false (3 < 16)

✅ second_space → index 18 path_len = second_space − path_start = 18 − 4 = **15**
Substring: indices 4..17 = /path/to/file

Guard: version_len == 0 → false
Guard: version_len >= MAX_VERSION_LEN (16) → false ✅ version_len = 8 → "HTTP/1.1"
